steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to present BOTH the study and its methodology. The right answer must name what Yuan was investigating and HOW he investigated it (computer simulations of the mother duck's wake).

steps.title: Find the choice that covers both pieces
steps.content: Choice B states the research question (how ducklings save energy swimming behind their mother) and the methodology (Yuan used computer simulations to study the effect of the mother duck's wake). Both elements are present.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'A study revealed': gives findings only and never mentions the methodology (computer simulations of the wake). ✗
  • Choice B — 'used computer simulations to study the effect of the mother duck's wake': names the study's purpose AND the methodology. ✓
  • Choice C — 'studied the physics behind the fact': vague about method and presents a finding (forward push from waves) as a premise; no methodology stated. ✗
  • Choice D — 'discovered that ducklings are pushed': reports a result with a statistic but says nothing about how Yuan conducted the study. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
